items.title: OpenAI Confidentially Files for IPO on the Heels of SpaceX and Anthropic
items.summary: The ChatGPT-maker announced it has filed paperwork to go public, just a week after rival Anthropic took the same step.

items.title: Polymarket and Kalshi Say Influencer Partners Can’t Deny Election Results, Actually
items.summary: Social media posts questioning the integrity of LA’s mayoral election were labeled “paid partnerships.” Then Kalshi and Polymarket told creators to delete them.

items.title: Everything Apple Announced at WWDC 2026
items.summary: Updates include a new souped-up Siri, lots of iOS enhancements, and some inkling on how an AI partnership with Google has come to power Apple’s products.

items.title: As OpenAI files for IPO, Sam Altman’s eye-scanning company is doing layoffs, report says
items.summary: Tools for Humanity, Sam Altman's identify verification company, is reportedly struggling to generate revenue and will downsize its staff.

items.title: Apple’s WWDC AI demos looked more real after $250M false ad settlement
items.summary: The vibe of Apple's 2026 WWDC keynote felt like a spouse proudly listing all the honey-do-list items tackled. One subtle example: the many AI demos of someone standing, phone in hand.

items.title: OpenAI files confidentially for IPO, following Anthropic
items.summary: The filing comes a little more than a week after its main rival, Anthropic, also filed to go public, ramping up the race between the two AI firms.

items.title: Iran and Israel say they will pause strikes but warn of retaliation if ceasefire breached again
items.summary: Israel's PM says his country is holding fire "at the moment", after Iran's armed forces said they had stopped military action.

items.title: Iran's strike on Israel suggests the regime's sense of resilience is growing
items.summary: Iran's decision to risk jeopardising peace talks may reflect how its leaders view their current position, writes BBC Persian editor Amir Azimi.

items.title: At least 35 dead after major earthquake strikes southern Philippines
items.summary: The magnitude-7.8 quake triggered small tsunami waves in the Philippines, Indonesia and Japan.

items.title: This startup wants to reduce payment friction on prediction markets
items.summary: EDGE Markets is announcing two new products to solve payment issues in prediction markets, the company shared exclusively with CNBC.

items.title: Hong Kong's IPO boom is developing a performance problem
items.summary: As Hong Kong vies with Wall Street to be the top IPO market, a growing number of pre-debut runups turn sour after their listing.

items.title: Bitcoin is cratering, but a new Wall Street crypto hype is on the rise
items.summary: As bitcoin dropped to its lowest price since 2024, investors flock to a new type of crypto investment linked to the hyperliquid platforms, HYPE ETFs.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: The Memo: Spencer Pratt comes up short in Los Angeles, drawing hollow claims of fraud
items.summary: President Trump’s favored candidate to become Los Angeles mayor looks like he won’t even make the runoff — a reality that has provoked a barrage of unsubstantiated claims of fraud from the commander in chief. Votes are still being counted in the nation’s second-largest city, where a high proportion of ballots are customarily cast by…

items.title: Cryptocurrency exchange Bybit to open SpaceX tokenized IPO access

items.title: Scientists discover the brain chemical that helps you break bad habits
items.summary: Scientists have uncovered a key brain signal that helps us break old habits and adapt when circumstances suddenly change. By watching mice navigate a virtual maze, researchers found that disappointment—when an expected reward failed to appear—triggered a surge of the neurotransmitter acetylcholine, making the animals more likely to try a new strategy. When acetylcholine was blocked, the mice became less flexible and were more likely to stick with outdated choices.

items.title: What is space-time? A mystery at the heart of reality
items.summary: What if our biggest idea about reality is built on a hidden misunderstanding? A new philosophical look at space-time challenges the popular view that the past, present, and future all exist together in a timeless "block universe." The argument suggests that physicists may be blurring the difference between things that exist and things that merely occur, creating deep confusion about what space-time actually is.

items.title: Scientists may have debunked one of humanity's oldest habits
items.summary: Ancient grooves on human teeth, once hailed as evidence of tooth-picking, may simply be the result of natural wear, according to a new study of wild primates. The research also revealed that a common modern dental defect appears to be uniquely human, hinting that today's lifestyles may be reshaping our teeth in unexpected ways.

items.title: Some pterosaurs may have boasted bold iridescence
items.summary: A new analysis of a 120-million-year-old fossil suggests at least one pterosaur species shimmered in iridescent greens and magentas.

items.title: A drug may help people on GLP-1 meds preserve muscle
items.summary: In a clinical trial, an experimental antibody reduced lean-mass loss in people on a GLP-1 drug. Whether that improves health is unclear.

items.title: AI cracked an Erdős math problem. Now experts want guardrails
items.summary: The result is correct but challenges core norms of mathematics: checking proofs, crediting ideas and keeping research open to everyone.
